data.body:
PASS-1 COACH (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety) — VERDICT: NOT-APPLICABLE (no findings)

T11 is an overlay that fires only when the plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills existing data. This Pass-4 coach plan does neither:
- It is an ADDITIVE, in-pipeline LLM computation that runs AFTER Pass-3 over the surviving findings and emits advisory coaching ([{move_id, subject, finding_refs}]) shipped in the SAME result.
- It explicitly does NOT own any persisted artifact: the stable per-finding `id` is MINTED by the orchestrator (ca03) and the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is owned by db7b — this coach is a stated CONSUMER ("findings + coaching ship in ONE result so the reference resolves locally (no circular alignment)"), so there is no new persisted-format definition, no in-place rewrite of existing data, and no backfill of historical reviews.
- No DDL, no large-table migration, no dual-write window, no backfill batching — none of the T11 binary checks (online/expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write, rollback/data-loss) have a target here.

ANTI-FP applies: this is a non-persisted, per-review computation. T11 PASS not-applicable. (Note: if a later revision were to add a NEW persisted/replayed schema owned by THIS ticket — rather than consuming ca03/db7b's — T11 would re-trigger; that ownership boundary is currently clean.)

affirmations: T11 (not-applicable — no schema/persisted-format change and no data backfill in scope).
